us_medium_tanks: Sherman doctrine is general-purpose, with the 75mm keeping HE and smoke relevant; armor fights are often solved with fire control, not only gun power.
british_firefly: Firefly doctrine is a one-tank heavy-kill add-on inside a normal Sherman troop; it trades loader space and flexibility for anti-Panther/Tiger reach.
td_family: M10/M18/M36 doctrine is ambush, reserve, and shot discipline; open-topped visibility is traded against protection.
british_infantry_tanks: Churchill doctrine is assault, obstacle crossing, and specialist support; speed is secondary to survivability and terrain power.
british_cruisers: Cromwell doctrine is speed, reconnaissance, and exploitation; information access matters as much as armor.
light_tanks: Stuart doctrine is screening, liaison, and route knowledge; it is a sensor more than a duelist.
recovery: M32 doctrine is rear-area throughput; the vehicle is a mechanic with armor, not a combat tank.
